author: shakesomuch
text: Nonlinloc to quakeml - picks are not associated with any channels

author: shakesomuch
text:
Dear all

When I am trying to convert nonlinloc file to quakeml event file, phases from nlloc are not associated with chans in quakeml file, although nlloc file does have them associated.

original issue:  
[eqcorrscan/EQcorrscan#69](https://github.com/eqcorrscan/EQcorrscan/issues/69)

nlloc file:  
<https://drive.google.com/open?id=0BzMcKedS7yadaFRjZlFaYUpOQkU>

I am using the script as simple as:

```
import glob
import os
from obspy import Catalog,read_events

cat = Catalog()

path = 'nllocs/'
event_files = glob.glob(path + '*.hyp')

for i, event_file1 in enumerate(event_files):

    cat += read_events(event_file1)
    print "Reading", i+1, "of", len(event_files)
    
    for event in cat:
    
        event.write("events/"+str(event.origins[0].time)+".xml", format="QUAKEML")
```

Is the channel association something that should be in quakeml format? the website <https://quake.ethz.ch/quakeml/> is not working and I am unable to find this info.

Thanks,  
blaz

author: calum-chamberlain
text: I don't think NonLinLoc files contain the full channel information (in your example it only gives the single-letter component name, e.g. Z, N or E). I may be wrong in general, but when I have used NLL only the component has been there.

author: shakesomuch
text:
ok, thanks for clarification...this must be the problem then.

blaz

author: megies
text:
I agree with [@calum-chamberlain](https://github.com/calum-chamberlain), in general there's only the component code in there (Z, N, E, ...) and not the full channel code. Although in the example file added by [@claudiodsf](https://github.com/claudiodsf) I see full channel codes in the nlloc output hyp file. [@claudiodsf](https://github.com/claudiodsf) how did you get a full channel code in there?

But then again, even if you had the full channel code in there, you're still lacking network and location code for proper association.. so, I think it's not really doable.

Yep, I agree with closing this for now, although it's a real shame that NLLoc seems to not always make it possible to resolve the full SEED ID of used channels.

Still would be interested if [@claudiodsf](https://github.com/claudiodsf) can shed more light on this though.

author: trichter
text:
I think I needed to address a similar issue when I wrote the plugin for SeismicHandler evt files. Only component information and the station code is specified in the evt files. I think the best option is to get the relevant infos from the Inventory object.

If someone is attacking this issue here, maybe he can reuse some code. Also it would be nice to have the same names for the relevant keywords. I used `inventory=None, id_map=None, id_default='.{}..{}'` , but this could also be changed, because it is not yet released.

Link to the file is [here](https://github.com/obspy/obspy/blob/master/obspy/io/sh/evt.py).

author: megies

author: alomax
text:
Since the current NLL formats and source codes precede NetStaLocChan I see two options for accommodating NSLC:

Current i/o fields:  
Station name (char*64) - station name or code  
Instrument (char*32) - instument identification for the trace for which the time pick corresponds (i.e. SP, BRB, VBB)  
Component (char\*32) - component identification for the trace for which the time pick corresponds (i.e. Z, N, E, H)

author: megies
text: Thanks for the info [@alomax](https://github.com/alomax).
